title: pedicularis anthemifolia subsp. elatior
intro: Pedicularis anthemifolia subsp. elatior, also known as the tall lousewort, is a perennial plant species that belongs to the borage family (Boraginaceae). The tall lousewort has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. It is believed to have health benefits such as reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and promoting digestion, as well as exhibiting antioxidant and immunomodulatory effects. The plant can be prepared into various herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are typically consumed orally or used topically to treat various health conditions.
